title: Releases | Beehive.govt.nz
content:
## Independent Review into Kāinga Ora announced
The Government has announced an independent review into Kāinga Ora, fulfilling another of its first 100-day plan commitments.

## Prime Minister to travel to Australia
Prime Minister Christopher Luxon will travel to Sydney on 20 December to meet with Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese.

## Trade Minister travels to India and Singapore to advance key relationships
Trade Minister Todd McClay will make his first overseas visit to India and Singapore to meet with his trade counterparts and to advance New Zealand’s relationships.

## Ministers, Mayor and Chair agree to end Let’s Get Wellington Moving
An agreement to dissolve Let’s Get Wellington Moving has been reached by Infrastructure & Housing Minister Chris Bishop, Transport Minister Simeon Brown, Mayor of Wellington City Council Tory Whanau, and Chair of Greater Wellington Regional Council Daran Ponter.

## New Zealand accepts Vice Chair role at the upcoming World Trade Organisation
Trade Minister Todd McClay has accepted the role of Vice Chair for the World Trade Organisation’s 13th Ministerial Conference in Abu Dhabi next year.

## Attorney-General appointed King’s Counsel
Prime Minister Christopher Luxon announced today the appointment of Attorney-General, Hon Judith Collins, as King’s Counsel.

## Bill to repeal ‘Ute Tax’ passed
The Government has passed legislation repealing the ‘Ute Tax’ and Clean Car Discount for all vehicles registered after 31 December 2023.

## Minister commends dedication of Wing 371
Police Minister Mark Mitchell joined Police Commissioner Andrew Coster and Wing Patron Dame Therese Walsh to applaud graduating Police Wing 371.

## Government takes first steps towards pragmatic and sensible freshwater rules
Cabinet has agreed to replace the National Policy Statement for Freshwater Management 2020 (NPS-FM).

## Economic repair job underway
Data released today, showing GDP declined by 0.3 per cent in the September quarter, suggests economic conditions remain challenging, Finance Minister Nicola Willis says.
